back_translation: 'My soul loathes my life; I will give free expression to my complaint and speak in the bitterness of my soul.
I will say to God: Do not condemn me; show me why You contend against me.
Is it good for You to oppress me, to despise the work of Your hands, and to favor the schemes of the wicked?
Do You have eyes of flesh? Do You see as a man sees?
Are Your days like the days of a mortal, or Your years like the days of a man,
that You should seek out my iniquity and search for my sin,
though You know that I am not guilty, and no one can deliver from Your hand?
Your hands shaped me and formed me altogether, yet now would You destroy me?
Remember that You molded me like clay; will You now return me to dust?
Did You not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
You clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.
You granted me life and steadfast love, and Your care preserved my spirit.
Yet You hid these things in Your heart; I know that this was Your purpose:
If I sinned, You would watch me, and would not acquit me of my iniquity.
If I am guilty, woe to me! And even if I am righteous, I cannot lift my head, for I am full of shame and aware of my affliction.
If I lift my head up, You hunt me like a lion, and again show Your wondrous power against me.
You bring new witnesses against me and increase Your anger toward me; hardships assault me wave after wave.
Why then did You bring me out of the womb? I should have died before any eye saw me.
It would have been better if I had never been, carried from the womb straight to the grave.
Are not my days few? Cease, then; leave me alone, that I may have a little comfort,
before I go—never to return—to a land of darkness and the shadow of death,
a land of deep darkness like the blackness of night, of the shadow of death and disorder, where even the light is like darkness.'
